settings.heading: Jon Sarkin (1953–2024)
settings.text:
Jon Sarkin was an outsider artist whose prolific output spanned three decades following a catastrophic stroke in 1989. What began as an uncontrollable compulsion to draw evolved into a body of more than 20,000 works — dense, layered compositions in ink, marker, paint, and collage that drew comparisons to Jean-Michel Basquiat and Jean Dubuffet.

His work entered the collections of the Centre Pompidou, the deCordova Sculpture Park and Museum, and the American Visionary Art Museum. He was the subject of a bestselling biography, Shadows Bright as Glass by Amy Ellis Nutt, and was profiled in The New Yorker, The New York Times, NPR, and GQ.

He died in Gloucester, Massachusetts on July 19, 2024.

settings.heading: The Catalog Raisonné
settings.text: The catalog raisonné documents over 4,500 works by Jon Sarkin, with high-resolution images, detailed provenance, and scholarly metadata. It is the authoritative record of Jon's artistic output.

settings.heading: Advisory Board
settings.text:
Colin Rhodes — Distinguished Professor; Contributing Editor, Raw Vision
Tony Millionaire — Eisner Award–winning cartoonist
Dr. Alice Flaherty — Harvard Medical School / Massachusetts General Hospital

settings.heading: The Estate
settings.text:
The Jon Sarkin Estate is managed by Mark Henderson, Estate Manager.

For inquiries about Jon Sarkin's work, contact Mark Henderson at art@jonsarkin.com.

settings.text: The New Yorker · The New York Times · NPR · GQ · Raw Vision · Boston Globe

Jon Sarkin's work is represented by Artists Rights Society (ARS), New York.
